dominant_error_pattern: Student emphasized self-awareness for classwide social-emotional learning instruction though the consultation scenario centers perspective-taking and interpersonal understanding best aligned to social awareness priorities in evidence-based SEL curricula.
instructional_red_flags: Distractor A (self-awareness) is a valid CASEL domain but wrong priority for this context. Teach CASEL 5-domain framework with scenarios: self-awareness (internal emotions) suits individual counseling; social awareness (perspective, empathy) suits classwide SEL to build peer understanding. Show a classroom consultation vignette; have students identify the root problem (peer dynamics?) and select matching CASEL domain. Practice matching domain to instructional context.

dominant_error_pattern: Student substituted records and test battery lists for ICEL components, producing problem-solving headings that do not match the standardized Instruction–Curriculum–Environment–Learning characteristics framework teams reference in ecological assessment documentation.
instructional_red_flags: Distractor A lists assessment types (records, tests, styles), not ICEL. Teach ICEL mnemonic: Instruction (pacing, clarity, scaffolds), Curriculum (scope, match to ability), Environment (noise, materials, peer dynamics), Learning characteristics (cognitive profile, processing, prior knowledge). Create a worksheet: sort 20 classroom factors into ICEL buckets. Have students reorganize a problem-solving report using ICEL headers; compare to original non-standard structure.
